Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "NorthWind", "Victor Huang", "Pam Kowalski", or "chain of custody" (or similar) to find the key NorthWind correspondence. Expected findings: (a) Victor Huang's formal security requirements email to Catalina listing non-negotiable chain-of-custody conditions for Raj Patel's server lab, IT escort must supervise all packing/loading, direct transport with no warehouse stops, signed custody log, real-time GPS tracking; (b) Pam Kowalski's final warning email to David Chen stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue to support the account, equipment was loaded before the IT escort arrived and placed into overnight warehouse storage, piano was damaged during extraction, and MoveOps lacked timely follow-up, Pam mentions PathFinder Logistics as a competitor; (c) Catalina Dubois's April 14 email to Pam promising a comprehensive service recovery plan "within the week."
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Catalina" and "recovery plan" or "Catalina" and "Pam" (or similar) to verify whether Catalina ever sent the promised recovery plan after April 14. Expected finding: no follow-up email from Catalina to Pam delivering the recovery plan, it was never sent.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "KeyMove", "Craig Nguyen", "piano", or "Steinway" (or similar) to find the specialty mover's damage documentation. Expected finding: email from craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com to Blessing Okafor on April 11 with damage photos documenting a surface scratch approximately 4.5-5 inches on the bass side rim near the keyboard cheek block of Emilia Cruz's Steinway Model B grand piano. Damage occurred during extraction at the second-floor landing because the turn out of the walkup was tighter than the access assessment indicated.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "SentinelChain", "Raj Patel", "supervised load", "escort confirmed", or "IT escort" (or similar) to find the vendor dispatch confirmation for the Raj Patel server lab pickup. Expected finding: email (email_email_05dac2433dbf) from dispatch@sentinelchain-logistics.com to fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com (CC: catalina.dubois@moveops.com) confirming the supervised pickup for Raj Patel at NorthWind's server lab, crew booked for Thursday April 9, IT/security escort to meet on site at 9:00 AM before any equipment is handled, crew to hold until escort sign-in is complete. This email establishes the original escort schedule that was violated when the SentinelChain crew arrived at 7:00 AM on April 10 and loaded without waiting for the escort, and is part of the evidence trail Chloe references in her ops timeline reconstruction.
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "NorthWind", "Raj Patel", "chain of custody", "Emilia Cruz", or "piano" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the NorthWind failures. Expected findings: (a) Chloe Vance's ops assessment in #operations noting chain-of-custody was completely new for MoveOps, flagging the Raj Patel move as complicated and a risk given they would be building the chain-of-custody process from scratch; (b) Blessing Okafor's admission in #operations (April 12) that she underestimated the turn radius on the Emilia Cruz walkup and should have required a pre-move site survey before the Steinway extraction, "that's on me"; (c) Catalina's internal account update to David about both NorthWind moves.
OE 6: Search CRM using crm_search_companies with "NorthWind" (or similar) to find the NorthWind Technologies company record (company_northwind), then use crm_list_engagements with the company ID to retrieve engagement history. Expected findings: (a) NorthWind Technologies record showing Customer type, 5 relocations, quality/timeline issues, Catalina Dubois as account manager; (b) engagement eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context with account recovery context and background on Q1 failures (5/10 CSAT, apartment quality issues, slow response times). Note: the Julian Brooks call log (see OE 7) is not linked to the NorthWind company record in CRM and will not appear in a company-filtered engagement search.
OE 7: Discover Julian Brooks's Emilia Cruz support call log in CRM. This engagement (crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c) has no company association in CRM, so the agent must find it through a broader approach, either by calling crm_list_engagements without a company filter and scanning results, or by cross-referencing Julian's Slack message in #customer-support (April 13, noting Emilia's call from the prior day) or the Linear ticket description which references it. Expected finding: Julian Brooks logged an inbound support call from Emilia Cruz on April 12 at approximately 10:05 AM PT. Emilia requested a same-day callback from someone who could speak to next steps, liability, and repair coverage. Julian told her the concern would be logged immediately, but no follow-up was ever assigned or completed.
OE 8: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind Technologies") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) for NorthWind Technologies relocation records. Expected findings: (a) Raj Patel record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) showing Status "In Progress," origin Chicago, destination Denver, with chain-of-custody requirements documented but no note about the breach; (b) Raj Patel audit note record (recRajPatelAuditNote0411) documenting "Escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, movers arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting"; (c) Emilia Cruz record (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver) showing Status "In Progress," Steinway piano details, but no note about the actual damage that occurred.
OE 9: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with queries related to "NorthWind" or "retention" (or similar) to find existing tracking tickets. Expected finding: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 ("NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations"), assigned to David Chen, priority P1, due April 24, which references Victor Huang's breach email, Chloe's reconstructed ops timeline, Julian's support note about Emilia's unanswered callback, Catalina's April 14 commitment, and the documentation integrity gap.
OE 10: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with queries related to "Raj Patel", "DOT", "NorthWind", or "custody" (or similar) to find the DOT documentation compliance ticket for the Raj Patel server lab move. Expected finding: issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind ("DOT Docs, Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS"), assigned to Chloe Vance, state_id: state_done, which claims the chain-of-custody log includes a signed placarding confirmation as part of the supervised loading documentation and that all four documentation categories are complete and on file. Cross-reference with Chloe Vance's April 12 Slack message in #operations which explicitly states the chain-of-custody tracking ticket tied to Raj is sitting as Done even though there is no signed custody log attached anywhere she can find, and that the ticket language says the supervised loading documentation and signed handoff package are on file but they are not. This ticket was falsely closed without the critical custody documentation ever being completed -- a key part of the prompt's investigation into the documentation integrity gap.
OE 11: Look up David Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts with query "David Chen" (or similar). Expected finding: david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 12: Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) to correct records that don't reflect what actually happened. Raj Patel's record should be updated with notes documenting the chain-of-custody breach (movers arrived at 7:00 AM, 2 hours before the 9:00 AM escort, equipment loaded without escort supervision, placed in overnight warehouse storage in violation of the no-warehouse requirement). Emilia Cruz's record should be updated with notes documenting the piano damage (4.5-5 inch scratch on bass side rim during stairwell extraction, KeyMove's report, Blessing's walkup assessment underestimated the turn radius).
OE 13: Add a comment on the NorthWind retention Linear ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1) using linear_create_comment with the complete operational timeline findings: server lab chain-of-custody breach details (movers 2 hours early, no IT escort, overnight warehouse), piano damage details from KeyMove (Craig Nguyen's report), Blessing's walkup assessment error, Catalina's recovery plan promised April 14 but never delivered, and Julian's unanswered Emilia Cruz callback from April 12.
OE 14: Send email using send_email from chloe.vance@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com with the full operational breakdown for the retention response. Content should cover: (a) Raj Patel server lab, movers arrived 7:00 AM, escort was scheduled for 9:00 AM, equipment loaded without supervision, servers placed in overnight warehouse storage violating NorthWind's explicit no-warehouse requirement; (b) Emilia Cruz piano, KeyMove extracted the Steinway, scratch caused by tight turn radius at second-floor landing that Blessing's walkup assessment underestimated, Craig Nguyen documented 4.5-5 inch scratch on bass side rim; (c) Catalina's April 14 recovery plan promise to Pam was never delivered; (d) Julian logged Emilia's April 12 support call requesting same-day callback but no one on our side followed up; (e) Airtable records still show both moves as "In Progress" with no documentation of the failures.
OE 15: Create CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement for NorthWind Technologies documenting the complete operational findings, chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel server lab move, piano damage on Emilia Cruz move with KeyMove's damage report, Catalina's undelivered recovery plan, Julian's unanswered Emilia callback, corrected Airtable records, and Pam Kowalski's final warning citing PathFinder Logistics as a competitor alternative.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 15: Create CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement for NorthWind Technologies documenting the complete operational findings, chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel server lab move, piano damage on Emilia Cruz move with KeyMove's damage report, Catalina's undelivered recovery plan, Julian's unanswered Emilia callback, corrected Airtable records, and Pam Kowalski's final warning citing PathFinder Logistics as a competitor alternative.
